data.title: commercetools and Stripe partnership aids enterprises in AI-driven commerce, for faster sales via AI agents. BOSTON, Dec. 11, 2025 - PRNewswire.
data.description: A recent partnership has been established in Boston on December 11, 2025, aiming to streamline the process of discovering agents, facilitating checkouts, and managing payments for enterprises. This collaboration allows businesses to generate additional revenue through a unified integration. The use of AI in commerce is rapidly emerging as a significant avenue for growth, with millions of opportunities arising in this sector.

data.title: Roche's oral drug for breast cancer reduces recurrence by 30% in early-stage patients. Positive study results presented at 2025 Symposium.
data.description: Roche Holdings AG reported data from the phase 3 lidERA Breast Cancer study, which investigated giredestrant as adjuvant endocrine therapy for ER-positive, HER2-negative, early-stage breast cancer. The findings were presented at the 2025 San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium. According to a press release on Wednesday, Roche stated that Giredestrant is the first oral SERD to exhibit superior iDFS in the adjuvant setting. LidERA marks the second positive phase 3 outcome for giredestrant, following the evERA Breast Cancer results. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Rivian debuts custom silicon, next-gen autonomy platform, and deep AI integration at Autonomy & AI Day in Palo Alto.
data.description: Rivian made headlines by revealing major advancements in automotive technology during its Autonomy & AI Day held in Palo Alto. The company, listed on NASDAQ as RIVN, introduced its own specialized silicon and presented its plans for enhancing vehicle autonomy in the future. Additionally, Rivian showcased a new software structure that leverages artificial intelligence. The event attracted attention for the innovative developments announced, setting the stage for the company's next phase of growth and technology integration.

data.title: NDF achieves significant international milestones with over USD 40 billion in assistance in 50 years.
data.description: The Minister of Tourism and Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Saudi Fund for Development has officially opened the third day of the MOMENTUM 2025 Development Finance Conference in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The event took place on December 11, 2025.

data.title: BPCE and Generali have decided to halt negotiations about a Joint Venture in Asset Management.
data.description: BPCE and Generali have decided to discontinue discussions regarding their plans to create a Joint Venture in Asset Management. The collaboration, which aimed to enhance their asset management offerings, has been called off by mutual agreement. This decision comes after thorough deliberation, with both parties opting to cease negotiations. The establishment of a Joint Venture was expected to combine BPCE's assets of approximately $500 billion with Generali's assets under management of around $600 billion. Despite initial intentions, the two organizations have opted to end the talks without proceeding with the proposed venture.

data.title: Xoriant recognized as Microsoft Fabric Featured Partner for AI analytics. Milestone highlights leadership in delivering unified insights.
data.description: Dec. 11, 2025 - Xoriant, a top digital engineering firm, has been named a Microsoft Fabric Featured Partner, showcasing its strength in providing combined analytics, AI-driven insights, and other services. This recognition highlights Xoriant's leadership in the industry.
Company buys back 500k of its own shares, representing 2% of total outstanding shares.

data.title: $10.68B eSIM market in 2024 to exceed $21.75B by 2030. G+D, Infineon, and Thales lead. Allows easy operator switch.
data.description: The eSIM market is projected to witness significant growth, with a value of over USD 10.68 billion in 2024 and expected to surpass USD 21.75 billion by 2030, showcasing a robust CAGR of 12.85% between 2025 and 2030. Key market players such as Giesecke+Devrient GmbH, Infineon Technologies AG, and Thales play a dominant role, providing consumers with the flexibility to switch mobile operators seamlessly, consequently heightening competition among network operators. Thales Group in particular stands out for its expertise in digital solutions.

data.title: Corporate directors focus on strategic execution, technology, and people in 2026; NACD survey projects modest growth.
data.description: A recent survey conducted by the National Association of Corporate Directors (NACD) indicates that corporate directors are anticipating a period of steady economic growth in the coming year. The survey highlights key areas of concern for directors, including a strong emphasis on effective execution of strategies, ensuring smooth CEO succession, enhancing workforce skills, and implementing technological advancements. These findings, gathered from directors across various industries, suggest a cautious yet optimistic outlook for 2026. The survey was conducted in December 2025 and underlines the importance of focusing on these key aspects to drive organizational success.

data.title: Eric Jackson identifies Nextdoor Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: NXDR) as his new '100-bagger' candidate. Stock climbs after his recommendation. Shares soared after his declaration, following similar rallies in other stocks.
data.description: The individual responsible for the remarkable retail surge in Opendoor Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: OPEN) has now pinpointed Nextdoor Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: NXDR) as his new prominent selection for a potential 100-fold increase. Eric Jackson ignited enthusiasm for NXDR as he labeled it "the most undervalued Agentic-AI platform of the 2020s." This endorsement led to a rapid escalation in NXDR's stock price, following similar movements seen in Opendoor and Better Home & Finance Holding Co. (NASDAQ: BETR) due to Jackson's influence. For more details, the entire article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
